title: Field bake: the observable-behavior gate. Proves the whole v1.5 release on the actual box with OBSERVABLE BEHAVIORS as the done-bars (not code sections): fresh /demo-up -> the local Directus observably SERVES the captured public catalog (data plane local, asset plane prod) curl-proven; /dev-up 2 --local-content same on an opt-in dev stack + N=0 stays prod-read; re-run everything twice (idempotency); the cold-start capture once; clean teardown reclaims the Directus container + port. Pre-pays the field-fix tail every prior release shipped after the fact.
position_note: M25 is the 5th + FINAL milestone of v1.5 'prop room' (M21->M25). M21 made the directus replay exit 0 + serve anonymously; M22 boots + verifies the per-stack instance as a compose service; M23 points services at it + closes referential gaps; M24 tells the corpus-wide truth + absorbs the hygiene strand; M25 PROVES it live on the real box. After this close: the release is complete -> the user runs /developer-kit:close-release.
kind: a LIVE field-bake section milestone — its done-bars were real stack runs (/demo-up, /dev-up 2 --local-content, re-runs, cold-start capture, teardown), not code sections. The rosetta m25/field-bake branch is 100% markdown/text: the field-bake log (overview/progress/decisions/spec-notes) + 2 curl serve-evidence files (db1/db2-serve-evidence.txt) + 2 skill arg-hint one-line fixes (KB-1/KB-2 from the Phase-0b kb-fidelity audit) + the close review records. The field-bake's CODE fixes (3 real release bugs the clean-room unit passes couldn't see) all land in the ext repo rosetta-extensions (stack-snapshot + demo-stack), already hardened + tagged prop-room-m25 @ 1a2fd91. The ext tag is moved by the orchestrator; close-milestone's merge is entirely rosetta-side.

sections.list: DB-1 fresh /demo-up local Directus SERVES (curl, offset 18055) GREEN · DB-2 /dev-up 2 --local-content SERVES (curl, offset 28055) + N=0 stays prod-read GREEN · DB-3 re-run idempotency GREEN · DB-4 cold-start capture EXERCISED (the sanctioned primary-read fill that surfaced + closed the tenant-leak) GREEN · DB-5 clean teardown reclaims the Directus container + port GREEN

field_bugs_surfaced.note: The field-bake earned its keep — 3 real release bugs surfaced live that clean-room unit passes couldn't see, all fixed Fate-1 in their owning ext module (stack-snapshot), then re-proven by re-run: (1) the directus_files referenced-subset capture OVER-captured 158 tenant-referenced files (M25-D5) — the tenant-data FIREWALL caught it FAIL-CLOSED (zero data written); the closure now appends AND NOT (TenantReferencedFilesFilter), the firewall AssertPlan now REQUIRES referenced-subset tables to declare + embed their tenant-exclusion (firewall NEVER weakened); captured directus_files 1417->1257. (2) directus_collections.group self-FK to an admin-UI folder collection outside the served set (M25-D6, apply-side SQLSTATE 23503) — the serve-row render NULLs group. (3) directus_files.folder/uploaded_by/modified_by FKs to uncaptured admin/library/users tables (M25-D7, replay-side) — a general TableSpec.NullColumns mechanism NULLs them. The complete-enumeration is proven (the per-stack content tables ship PRIMARY KEYS only, 0 content-table FKs, so the only live FK constraints a replay can violate are the bootstrap-created directus_files ones). All three nulled columns are admin-UI/authoring-identity only — zero effect on serving the asset (resolves by id->storage/filename_disk). PLUS a 4th, offline-build bug (M25-D1): M24's Go pin broke the offline clerkenstein cross-compile (GOPROXY=off GOSUMDB=off can't fetch+verify the toolchain module) -> /demo-up aborted; fixed with GOTOOLCHAIN=local (demo-stack).

tests.language: none rosetta-side (the m25/field-bake branch is markdown/text only — the field-bake log + 2 evidence files + 2 skill arg-hint fixes + the close records carry no executable code). The ext code fixes' tests live in the rosetta-extensions repo (out of this merge's scope), hardened in a dedicated pass.

(The headline 850 is the rosetta-extensions module aggregate as of M24; the +8 deepen existing M25-fix functions without adding new Test/Fuzz func-decls beyond the 8 cited.)

All M25 milestone-doc + frontend-tier cross-references resolve. The Phase-0b kb-fidelity audit had already confirmed every v1.5 topic->doc->code triple ALIGNED.

review.decision_triage: M25-D2 (the 16 GB-host VM ceiling — 12 GB fails to boot, ~10 GB max, full UI tier can't co-reside) BLENDED (#M25-D2) into corpus/ops/demo/frontend-tier.md § the 12 GB Docker-VM prerequisite — a real operator-facing truth. M25-D8/M25-D9 (the close re-fates of the full-UI Playwright render + the dev-2 taxonomy rc=4) -> ARCHIVE (maintainer-only fate records). M25-D1/D3/D4/D5/D6/D7 -> ARCHIVE — ext-code / implementation specifics whose behavioral CONTRACTS are already documented in corpus (firewall fail-closed + public-only in safety.md; structure-bearing capture in snapshot-spec.md / snapshot-cold-start.md; the exit-0/4 split in directus-local.md), confirmed ALIGNED by the Phase-0b audit. Full options-considered lists stay in decisions.md.
review.deferral_audit: GREEN — audit-deferrals/deferral-audit-2026-06-13-m25-close.md. The STRONGEST outcome for the one inherited item whose destination WAS this milestone: DEF-M21-02 (serve-live-integration harness) RESOLVED Fate-1 in M25 — the serve-row render was driven against a real structure-bearing capture on a live per-stack Directus and observably serves (DB-1 + DB-2 curl-proven), dropping off the ledger. 2 standing items unchanged + not aged (DEF-M21-01 replayCmd conn-seam, untouched; DEF-M10-01 S3 blob bytes, refs-only re-confirmed by DB-1). 2 M25-originated env items fated fresh, both single/not-repeat/not-aged: M25-DEF-01 full-UI Playwright render -> DROP-as-v1.5-deliverable/environmental backlog (host-budget constraint, behavior proven by curl; M25-D8); M25-DEF-02 dev-2 taxonomy rc=4 -> KEEP tracked dev migrate-ordering follow-up (non-fatal, orthogonal to content-serve; M25-D9). 0 repeat, 0 chronic, 0 aged-out, 0 blockers.

notes: M25 is the 5th + FINAL milestone of v1.5 'prop room' — the live field-bake observable-behavior gate that pre-pays the field-fix tail every prior release shipped after the fact. It earned its keep: all 5 done-bars GREEN, and the operator-sanctioned prod read (the postgres MCP marco_read primary-read DSN) filled the cache structure-bearing so the local Directus observably SERVES the captured public catalog (curl-proven, data plane local + asset plane prod). The live runs surfaced + fixed 4 real release bugs the clean-room unit passes couldn't see — most importantly the directus_files tenant-data-leak (M25-D5) that the FIREWALL CAUGHT FAIL-CLOSED (zero data written) and the fix closed in the FILTER (the firewall was never weakened) — plus two dangling-FK-to-admin-UI-table classes (M25-D6/D7) and the offline-build GOTOOLCHAIN regression from M24's Go pin (M25-D1). The ext harden added +8 tests (firewall AssertPlan -> 100%). Close was clean: 3 findings (1 scope checkbox nit / 0 code / 1 docs / 0 tests / 1 blend + 5 archive), the deferral audit GREEN with the strongest outcome (DEF-M21-02 RESOLVED in its destination milestone), 0 escape-hatch. The rosetta merge is markdown/text only (the code fixes are in ext, tagged prop-room-m25 @ 1a2fd91). Next: v1.5 is complete -> the user runs /developer-kit:close-release for the release-level review + the release/01.50-prop-room -> main merge + tag.
